summary: Created follow-up page template at /hotline/demo with copy-to-clipboard, mobile-first dark theme design, IntersectionObserver animations, and clipboard fallback for mobile. Page generation template source included for D2.2 integration.
broadcast: Page template component source is in the deposit under 'page_template_source'. D2.2 should use generateFollowUpPageSource() with call data to produce a React component string, then pass it to update_space_route via /zo/ask. The slug utility is in 'slug_utility'. The component is self-contained — all data is embedded via JSON.stringify, no API calls needed at render time. Route must be created with public=true. Community links default to Discord + support.zocomputer.com if not provided.

integration_guide.step_1: After VAPI call analysis completes, extract: callerName, summary, pathway, level, primaryInterest, prompts[], nextSteps[]
integration_guide.step_3: Generate page source: generateFollowUpPageSource(data) → full React component string
integration_guide.step_4: Call /zo/ask with prompt: 'Create a public zo.space page route at /hotline/<slug> with route_type page and public true. The code is: <source>'
integration_guide.step_5: Update DuckDB calls table: SET followup_url='https://va.zo.space/hotline/<slug>', followup_sent=true, followup_sent_at=NOW()
integration_guide.step_6: Send SMS to caller with the URL

learnings: zo.space page routes support IntersectionObserver and navigator.clipboard APIs — no polyfills needed
JSON.stringify handles escaping for data embedded in JS source (backticks and template literals in string values are safe since they're inside regular quotes, not template literals)
Clipboard API requires HTTPS which zo.space provides; included execCommand fallback for edge cases
Tailwind 4 on zo.space supports all standard utility classes including animation, backdrop-blur, gradient stops
